items.title: CPI inflation soars by most since 2022 as gas prices biteHeadline inflation clocked in 3.3% higher than a year ago, a rapid acceleration from February's levels.
Stock futures stall with CPI data, Iran talks in focus
TSMC sales smash record as war fails to dent AI demand
Has Hormuz reopened after Trump's demands? Where things stand.

Why the Meta Muse Spark rally is continuing

Inflation rose 0.9% in March amid energy price shocksConsumer prices saw a rise of 0.9% month-over-month that was in line with estimates, as reported by the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), while inflation rose 3.3% year-over-year (vs. estimates of 3.4%). Core CPI — which excludes food and energy costs — rose by 0.2% monthly (vs. estimates of 0.3%) and 2.6% annually (vs. estimates of 2.7%). Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland break down the fresh inflation data, taking into account the massive energy and oil (CL=F, BZ=F) price shocks experienced over this last month.
Mortgage rates fall on Iran ceasefire: What it means for home sales
Inflation from Iran, oil shocks won't be going away anytime soon

US inflation surges to 3.3% as Iran war fuels energy price shockUS consumer prices rose sharply in March, with headline inflation climbing to 3.3% year-on-year as energy costs spiked in the wake of the Iran war, official data showed on Friday.View on euronews
CPI report shows inflation surges to 3.3% in March amid Iran war energy shockAmericans had already been navigating persistent cost-of-living pressures before last month's energy shock
Inflation surged in March as Iran war drove up energy costsInflation rose at an annual rate of 3.3% in March, driven by the sharpest monthly increase in gas prices since 1967.
Inflation Rose to 3.3% in March, Driven by Rising Fuel CostsThe rise in consumer prices was in line with expectations, but much hotter than February’s gain of 2.4%.
Inflation soars by most since 2022 as gas prices biteHeadline inflation clocked in 3.3% higher than a year ago, while being up 0.9% on a monthly basis, a rapid acceleration from February's levels.

Gap between high-income households' wage growth and everyone else is at its widest since 2015After-tax wage growth is accelerating faster among higher-income households as other households trail behind, a new Bank of America Institute report shows.

IMF head says global growth outlook is lower due to war in IranInternational Monetary Fund managing director Kristalina Georgieva said Thursday that the fund has lowered its global growth outlook even in the most optimistic scenario on account of the conflict in the Middle East.

Fed's March minutes: Officials saw Iran war increasing inflation, delaying rate cutsFederal Reserve officials expected that higher oil prices resulting from the Iran war would increase inflation in the near term and delay price inflation falling back toward their 2% goal, according to minutes from the central bank’s March policy meeting released Wednesday.

Dallas Fed study: Inflation expectations could improve quickly if Strait of Hormuz reopensWith a two-week ceasefire underway in Iran, oil prices are dropping. If the situation holds, expectations for higher inflation could also drop.

Fed's vice chair says oil price shock complicates inflation outlookFederal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson said Tuesday that the jolt in energy prices complicates his inflation outlook, and depending on how long the Middle East conflict lasts, elevated oil prices could weigh on consumer and business spending.

analysis.analysis:
---
## MACRO ENVIRONMENT SUMMARY
- DXY at 98.659, -0.16% today, -1.37% 5-day — Dollar in a clear 5-day downtrend. Sustained weakness below 100 is a direct tailwind for equities and risk assets. Not a one-day event.
- VIX at 19.18, -1.59% today, -19.65% 5-day — Fear has been systematically unwinding all week. Down nearly 20% in 5 days. Institutional hedges being removed, not added. Below 20 = risk-on regime officially restored.
- 10Y Yield at 4.303, +0.23% today — Yields ticking up slightly on the CPI print, but -0.23% over 5 days. The week trend is stable/down. Today's tick is noise unless it accelerates.
- Gold at $4,796, +0.08% today, +3.11% 5-day — Still bid on geopolitical uncertainty (Iran war premium). Not collapsing despite the ceasefire, which means markets aren’t fully pricing in resolution yet.
- Crude Oil at $98.03, +0.16% today, -12.11% 5-day — Oil down 12% in 5 days — the ceasefire is working its way through energy prices. Still elevated vs pre-war levels but the directional move is deflationary. Critical: if Hormuz fully reopens, oil drops further and equity rally extends.
Environment: RISK-ON — VIX collapsed below 20, DXY weakening, oil retreating from war highs. Bull regime restored for the week.

## KEY NEWS ANALYSIS
**CPI came in as expected with core beating estimates:** Headline 3.3% YoY (in-line), Core 2.6% YoY (beat 2.7% est), Core MoM 0.2% (beat 0.3% est). This is the key event of the day. A beat on headline would have been hawkish and killed the rally. Instead, core staying contained tells the Fed the Iran energy shock has not spread into broader inflation — removes the rate-hike tail risk entirely.

**Iran ceasefire/truce active but fragile:** Multiple headlines confirm a 2-week ceasefire is underway. Russian-flagged tanker transiting Hormuz — signs of reopening. Dallas Fed study explicitly says inflation expectations would improve quickly if Hormuz fully reopens. This is the macro event that drove the entire week’s +4-5% rally in both NQ and ES.

**AI/Tech sector carrying the market:** TSMC revenue +35% (record quarter, “war fails to dent AI demand”), CoreWeave/Anthropic deal announced, Meta Muse Spark rally continuing, chip names leading. NQ is the primary beneficiary — AI demand is structural, not sentiment-driven.

**Dow Transports at record high:** Classic Dow Theory confirmation signal. When transports confirm industrials, it validates the broader bull move. Bullish structural signal.

**8 consecutive up sessions:** Futures gaining for 8th straight day. Powerful momentum but also stretched — “relief rally hits pause” is a live risk intraday.

Overall News Sentiment: BULLISH with geopolitical wildcard

## ECONOMIC EVENTS IMPACT
- **CPI 3.3% YoY (in-line) / Core 2.6% (beat)** — Bullish. Removes hawkish Fed surprise risk. Market reaction: relief, not euphoria — which is sustainable.
- **Core CPI MoM 0.2% vs 0.3% est** — Most important number. Month-over-month core deceleration = oil shock contained. Fed can stay patient.
- **Michigan Consumer Sentiment Prel (est. 52 vs 53.3 prior)** — Not yet released. Declining trend. A weak print (below 50) could cap the upside or trigger an intraday dip. Watch at release time.
- **Factory Orders MoM (est. -0.2%)** — Expected negative. If in-line, ignored. Significantly worse = minor headwind.

## MARKET TREND CONTEXT
- **NQ:** 5 consecutive UP candles. Price 25,310 — already printing above prior day high (25,269). Off week low of 23,666 by +7%. Today’s range 25,193–25,386 with price holding near highs. Sellers have had no control all week.
- **ES:** 5 consecutive UP candles. Price 6,872 — today’s high 6,888 already above prior week high of 6,876. Breaking out into new weekly high territory. ES showing slightly less momentum than NQ (AI/tech drag) but structurally identical.

## PREDICTION
**NYSE SESSION SIDE: BUY SIDE**
**Confidence: 68% — MODERATE**

**NQ Prediction: BULLISH**
**ES Prediction: BULLISH**

## TOP REASONS
1. **Core CPI beat (0.2% vs 0.3% est)** — The only number that mattered today came in clean. No upside inflation surprise means no hawkish Fed pivot risk. Market had priced in this range; the beat removes the one scenario that would have reversed the week’s gains.
2. **VIX -19.65% over 5 days, now below 20** — The fear unwind is structural. Institutional desks removing hedges, not adding. Below 20 VIX is a regime-level change — algorithms and risk models flip to risk-on positioning, creating self-reinforcing buy pressure.
3. **Price structure: 5 consecutive UP candles, both instruments above prior highs** — Clean breakout structure. NQ above prior day high, ES above prior week high. Trend is UP on every timeframe. Momentum has not broken.

## KEY RISKS
- **Iran truce collapses** — Any headline of resumed hostilities spikes oil and VIX instantly. Single biggest risk to the entire bullish thesis. Would flip both instruments to sell within minutes.
- **Michigan Consumer Sentiment comes in well below 50** — Sentiment trending down (53.3 → est. 52). A shock print below 50 signals consumer capitulation and gives bears a narrative catalyst for a session reversal.
- **8 consecutive up sessions = crowded longs** — At this point every dip-buyer from the past 2 weeks is in profit. A single bad headline or failed breakout triggers cascading profit-taking. The move is extended — not wrong, but not clean.

## WHAT TO WATCH DURING NYSE SESSION
- **Michigan Sentiment release** — if print comes below 50, expect a sharp intraday pullback. If in-line or above 52, ignored.
- **Iran/Hormuz headlines** — Any news of truce breakdown = immediate sell. Confirmation of Hormuz reopening = leg higher.
- **NQ key levels:** Support 25,251 (prev close) → 25,193 (today’s low). Resistance 25,386 (today’s high) → 25,500 round number. A hold above 25,251 on any dip = buy. Loss of 25,193 = caution.
- **ES key levels:** Support 6,863 (prev close) → 6,850 (today’s low). Resistance 6,888 (today’s high) → 6,900 (round number). Break above 6,900 = momentum continuation signal.
- **Oil:** Staying below $100 = risk-on intact. Any spike back above $102 = geopolitical escalation, sell equities.
- **VIX:** Hold below 20 = bull regime. Break above 21 intraday = hedges being added, caution.

## ONE-LINE SUMMARY
“2026-04-10 NYSE SESSION: BUY SIDE — Core CPI beat removes Fed hawkish risk, VIX collapsed 20% on the week below 20, 5 consecutive UP candles on NQ and ES with price above prior highs; Iran ceasefire fragility and 8-session extended rally are the sole risks to an otherwise clean bullish structure.”
---
